text:
There once was an old man named Ernest. He wasn't particularly famous or wealthy. One day while walking his little rabbit hole he found himself watching birds swooping downward. They seemed intent on capturing something.

Ernest knew it could be worms. So he threw a worm outside. The bird descended. Ernest threw another. The bird took that.

"Oh no," Ernest thought. Perhaps nets are better. He brought out one big one.

That night the birds hovered. Nothing happened.

Perhaps they knew something he didn't?

Ernest stayed quiet. The quiet kind of listening maybe?

The next morning everything was still. Nothing moved. There were no songs.

Ernest went closer. Everything was twisted.

That hawk! That rabbit!

No. There must be something.

He looked deeper. A hole. Beneath a leaf.

Oh!

Just mice. But alive.

Ernest.

Ernest knew.

He wanted them. That's kind.

Every night.

He took out his flute.

And maybe they
